title: NOC 13100 – Administrative Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Administrative Officers play a central role in managing office operations, government services, institutional admissions, and organizational administration across Canada. Classified under NOC 13100, this occupation remains in strong demand nationwide and offers practical immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker programs and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training

common_job_titles: Access to information and privacy officer Administrative officer Administrative services coordinator Forms management officer Office administrator Office manager Office services coordinator Planning officer Records analyst – access to information Surplus assets officer University admissions officer Communications service coordinator Exam invigilator – post-secondary institution Exam supervisor – post-secondary institution Implementation officer Liaison officer Public trustee Regional services chief Relocation commissioner
duties: Oversee office procedures and implement new administrative systems
Set priorities, delegate tasks, and ensure deadlines are met
Manage admissions processes for post-secondary institutions
Administer access-to-information and privacy legislation requests
Coordinate accommodation, relocation, security, parking, and asset disposal services
Conduct budgeting, contracting, and project-planning analyses
Assist with operating budgets and inventory controls
Prepare reports, manuals, and official correspondence
employment_requirements: College diploma or university degree in business or public administration (often required)
Experience in senior clerical or executive support roles
Project management certification for some employers
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 97,900 new positions for Administrative Officers, compared with 87,800 job seekers—indicating favorable prospects for skilled overseas applicants.
additional_information: Administrative Officers are employed across federal and provincial governments, educational institutions, healthcare organizations, and private corporations.

Sustained hiring across public and private sectorsGrowing demand for compliance and records-management rolesEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported immigration optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $36,600 to $80,900 CAD
